canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Path patching is consistently characterized as a causal intervention and mechanistic interpretability method for identifying influential components within a neural network. The technique operates by replacing, or "patching," the internal activations of specific components, most commonly attention heads, from a reference model run into a counterfactual run with a different input. By measuring the effect of this intervention on the final output, researchers aim to identify components with direct causal effects on model predictions. The prevailing use of path patching is to discover computational "circuits" and localize decision-making for various capabilities. For instance, papers report using it to find the circuits for indirect object identification and entity tracking, identify heads responsible for mathematical computation, and localize components for commonsense reasoning. It is also applied to measure the importance of components for phenomena like sycophancy and option symbol bias. Some sources describe it as a "more surgical variant of activation patching," and it is sometimes used in conjunction with other methods like logit attribution.
papers: Circuit Component Reuse Across Tasks in Transformer Language Models
Fine-Tuning Enhances Existing Mechanisms: A Case Study on Entity Tracking
Towards Best Practices of Activation Patching in Language Models: Metrics and Methods
Causally Motivated Sycophancy Mitigation for Large Language Models
Differentiation and Specialization of Attention Heads via the Refined Local Learning Coefficient
HeadMap: Locating and Enhancing Knowledge Circuits in LLMs
The Same but Different: Structural Similarities and Differences in Multilingual Language Modeling
Towards Universality: Studying Mechanistic Similarity Across Language Model Architectures
AlgoPuzzleVQA: Diagnosing Multimodal Reasoning Challenges of Language Models with Algorithmic Multimodal Puzzles
You Only Read Once (YORO): Learning to Internalize Database Knowledge for Text-to-SQL

definitions.text: A more surgical variant of activation patching used to discover circuits by intervening on specific paths within the model's computation graph.
definitions.paper: Circuit Component Reuse Across Tasks in Transformer Language Models

definitions.text: A mechanistic interpretability method that identifies causal components in neural networks by measuring the effect of patching activations between different inputs, used here to discover the entity tracking circuit.
definitions.paper: Fine-Tuning Enhances Existing Mechanisms: A Case Study on Entity Tracking
